subject: Project Management
title: Lean in Banking
description: A 60-second lesson on Lean in Banking.

hook.text: Banks have some of the most convoluted processes in any industry. Lean is transforming them.

buildup.text: A typical mortgage application passes through 40 steps and 12 handoffs. Most are unnecessary.

discovery.text: Lean banking reduced mortgage processing time from 45 days to 15 at some institutions.

twist.text: Compliance regulations make banking hard to simplify — but lean distinguishes legal requirements from pure waste.

climax.text: Lean banking shows that even heavily regulated industries can strip out waste without breaking rules.

punchline.text: Regulation demands compliance, not complexity. Lean removes the rest.

quiz.question: What did lean achieve in mortgage processing?
options: Reduced processing time from 45 days to 15
Eliminated the need for credit checks
Doubled the number of required documents
